components.intent: Make phase motion honest by binding each band to a real semantic mode.

scenarios.label: Live transition
scenarios.why: Use only when the backend exposes a measured transition from one stage to the next.

scenarios.label: Reserved boundary
scenarios.why: Keep remediation visible without visually pretending that it is already wired.

components.intent: Present latency as a measured transition, not as generic progress.

scenarios.label: Real duration
scenarios.why: Only measured transition timing should render as elapsed latency.

scenarios.label: Static status
scenarios.why: Stable posture should render as a status chip, not a running meter.

components.intent: Show threshold proximity without implying elapsed time.

scenarios.label: Near trigger
scenarios.why: The bar should answer how close the same-key path is to becoming cluster-legible.

scenarios.label: Triggered
scenarios.why: Once the gate is crossed, wording must switch from proximity to reached-state.

components.intent: Make the active incident the first read on the page.

scenarios.label: Alert focus
scenarios.why: The header should answer what happened and why the operator is here before any overview grid.

scenarios.label: Compare focus
scenarios.why: Compare mode should keep the same current slice while the branches diverge underneath it.

scenarios.label: Collapsed surface
scenarios.why: The first-entry page can keep global runtime controls available without spending a full top band on always-open metadata.

scenarios.label: Immersive scroll handoff
scenarios.why: The first downward scroll should fully clear the floating dock and hand the viewport to the incident stage instead of leaving a dead spacer band.

components.intent: Allow first-entry users to switch the console shell between English and Chinese without losing the active incident context.

scenarios.label: English shell
scenarios.why: International review and conference narration should keep the current slice readable in English.

scenarios.label: Chinese shell
scenarios.why: Local operator review should be able to read the same incident story in Chinese without changing the underlying runtime selection.

components.intent: Turn the active incident into a tactical scene rather than a text block.

scenarios.label: Suggestion live scene
scenarios.why: The first screen should visibly show that the system has already moved from signal to guidance.

scenarios.label: Cluster watch scene
scenarios.why: When the cluster gate is still forming, motion should emphasize threshold proximity instead of fake completion.

components.intent: Keep evidence tied to suggestion fields instead of freeform explanation text.

scenarios.label: Full bundle
scenarios.why: When a suggestion exists, context, topology, change, and historical evidence must all stay queryable.

scenarios.label: Alert only
scenarios.why: When no suggestion exists, the UI should make the missing evidence bundle obvious rather than silently inventing one.

scenarios.label: Docked overlay
scenarios.why: On the first-entry page, the evidence surface should stay available but hidden behind an explicit expand action so the main stage keeps the screen.

components.intent: Treat each trigger cluster or suggestion as a replayable incident instead of mixing raw feed items into the main queue.

scenarios.label: Replayable incidents
scenarios.why: The queue should list historical problem incidents in reverse time order, and selecting one should pivot the whole page around that incident.

components.intent: Expand each top-level stage into a visible process chain with systems, facts, and reconstructed timeline support.

scenarios.label: Reconstructed action chain
scenarios.why: When a user clicks a stage, the UI should show the phase sequence, stage nodes, key facts, and related timeline points instead of only a short summary.

components.intent: Promote the story-and-visuals area into a primary stage for data and interaction structure.

scenarios.label: Expanded theater
scenarios.why: The story section should feel like a main operational canvas, not a small secondary panel hidden behind a timid disclosure.

scenarios.label: Transport degraded
scenarios.why: When CSS, SSE, or large assets are only partially available, the theater must degrade into a readable but still structured shell.

scenarios.label: Viewport takeover
scenarios.why: After the dock collapses, the incident stage should hold nearly the full viewport so the queue rail and projector become the primary read.

components.intent: Represent control posture without blurring observe/explain/act/feed-back.

scenarios.label: Manual handoff
scenarios.why: The operator should see that execution is available as a boundary, but not yet wired as an automatic stage.

scenarios.label: Guided execution
scenarios.why: Agent-enhanced runs should still show operator ownership, but with a structured path into action and feedback.

components.intent: Keep same-window branch deltas legible for review and conference prep.

scenarios.label: Rule only branch
scenarios.why: The baseline branch should be visually calm and explicit about what it does not provide.

scenarios.label: Agent enhanced branch
scenarios.why: The enhanced branch should highlight added coverage and its extra runtime cost in the same frame.

components.intent: Make replay and export readiness first-class outputs.

scenarios.label: Partial export
scenarios.why: Baseline runs should show what can be exported without pretending replay is complete.

scenarios.label: Replay ready
scenarios.why: Enhanced runs should surface that the branch can support replay, review, and figure generation.
